$MELI gets valued as an e-commerce business.

But zoom out.

Mercado Pago is becoming the first financial relationship for 122M unbanked adults across Latin America.
First account. First card. First loan.
Once you bank someone for the first time, you don't lose them.

Mercado Envios is building a logistics network across a continent with almost no existing infrastructure.
No Amazon. No FedEx. No UPS.
Just $MELI, building it from scratch.

The e-commerce is what you're paying for.

The fintech and logistics are what you're getting for free.

That's optionality.

Mercado Libre opened a logistics/fulfillment hub in China in Dec 2025. It lets Chinese sellers stock goods there for faster, cheaper shipping direct to Brazil & other LatAm countries (aimed at competing with Shein/Temu/AliExpress).

This does increase competition for Brazilian sellers of similar imported goods, potentially pressuring local prices/jobs (111k figure appears to reference platform-supported Brazilian sellers/roles, though exact impact isn't specified in reports).

The "extinction of small vendors" & "new monopoly" framing is opinionated hype—no evidence of total control or elimination. It's standard cross-border e-com expansion; consumers get benefits, local sellers face more rivalry. The Xi/Dilma meme adds drama but no data.

Argentina is undergoing major economic changes. The government stopped the central bank from printing money to cover debts. This strict policy helped lower inflation to nearly 30% in 2025. The economy is recovering, and real GDP is projected to grow between 3.5% and 4.0% in 2026.

The digital retail market in Argentina is growing rapidly. Forecasts predict the market could reach a total value of over US$50B by 2031. Most shoppers use mobile phones to make purchases instead of computers. Digital wallets now process nearly half of all online payments. The central bank built a payment system called Transferencias 3.0. This system lets different payment apps scan the same QR codes easily. It merges offline physical stores with online digital payments.

$MELI dominates this digital and financial market. This month, MercadoLibre announced a massive US$3.4B investment in Argentina. They are using this money to build new logistics centers and expand their fintech operations. To help users fight inflation, they can hold the Meli Dolar stablecoin. The coin also launched in Brazil, Mexico, and Chile. This digital asset allows users to preserve their wealth within the Mercado Pago ecosystem. MercadoLibre uses its loyalty programs and high-margin digital ads to keep users locked into its platform.

The Argentine government recently removed strict import limits. This change brings heavy competition from cheap Asian shopping platforms like Shein and Temu. MercadoLibre defends its market share by offering much faster local shipping and reliable returns. They are also injecting cheap Chinese goods through their recently launched Full China Strategy.

Ultimately, Argentina has vast physical resources like lithium and natural gas. This resource wealth combined with MercadoLibre's digital dominance creates a highly lucrative setup for long-term growth.

Aluminum moved at the Asia open. Exit on ceasefire or confirmation the UAE smelter is back online. Primary risk is demand destruction from a global slowdown outrunning the supply shock.

$MOS: the sulfur thesis deepened over the weekend with no price move yet. Gulf OSP sulfur is +163% YoY, 44–50% of seaborne sulfur transits Hormuz, and zero strategic reserves exist globally. Even post-ceasefire, Gulf refinery throughput runs below the pre-crisis baseline — the old supply level doesn't come back. MOS hold, moat got longer-dated.
